mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 320 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4%) combined with strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals inflationary pressure, prompting Fed rate hikes that reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where both UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) and GDP growth (GDPC1) indicate strong consumer demand, boosting corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint movement of RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to predict SPX returns with Rank IC > 0.15 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger supply-side capacity utilization, reducing input costs and improving corporate margins, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO shows no significant correlation with SPX returns in 1-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs, dampening equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) in a low-inflation environment signals a strong labor market, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of periods with UNRATE declines.
